_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
The EPA Clean Ports Program does not establish a producer, extended producer responsibility (EPR), or jurisdictional registration regime applicable to freight forwarders. There is no workspace registration obligation under this programme.

**Grant-recipient registration requirements (applicable to port-authority partners, not the workspace directly):**

Entities applying for or holding Clean Ports Program grants must register with and maintain an active account in the System for Award Management (SAM.gov) and obtain a Unique Entity Identifier (UEI) to submit applications through Grants.gov. These are standard federal grant pre-award requirements under 2 CFR Part 200.

Grant recipients draw down funds through the Automated Standard Application for Payments (ASAP.gov) platform administered by the U.S. Department of Treasury. Disbursements must occur within five business days of drawdown to comply with the Uniform Grants Guidance minimum-cash-need standard.

**Data fields grantees must collect and report (per EPA Form 5900-721 and the Guide for Vehicle, Equipment, and Infrastructure Activity Data Collection, June 2025):**
- Vehicle/equipment type, model, and deployment date
- Activity data (e.g., number of truck visits per year to port terminals)
- Whether battery energy storage systems (BESS) were charged by internal combustion generators vs. grid/solar power
- Scrappage eligibility and evidence documentation for replaced diesel equipment
- Technology deployment statements confirming ZE equipment is in service

**Published vs. promised guidance:**
- Quality Assurance Project Plan (QAPP) guides for both emissions inventories and vehicle/equipment activity data were published July 2025.
- Zero-Emission Technology Deployment Project Reporting Template (EPA Form 5900-721) was updated December 2025.
- Supplemental resource for completing progress reporting templates published January 2026 (EPA-420-B-26-005).
- No additional registration portals or workspace-facing reporting obligations have been announced as of May 2026.

*Source: Clean Ports Program: Grantee Resources, US EPA, March 2026. https://www.epa.gov/ports-initiative/clean-ports-program-grantee-resources; EPA Clean Ports Program: Guide for Vehicle, Equipment, and Infrastructure Activity Data Collection, US EPA, June 2025. https://www.epa.gov/system/files/documents/2025-07/ports-program-guide-activity-data-collection-2025-06.pdf*
